title: `read_csv_auto` fails to parse column as timestamp when meets YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm format
description:
### What happens?

Column that has mixed ISO 8601 timestamps with **YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm** timestamp being parsed BY `read_csv_auto` as `VARCHAR` instead of `TIMESTAMP`.

### To Reproduce

using 
`CREATE TABLE test AS SELECT * FROM read_csv_auto(%path to csv file%);
DESCRIBE test;`

---

time 
2021-10-11T18:59:14Z
2021-10-11T12:55Z

time column parsed as `VARCHAR`

---

time 
2021-10-11T18:59:14Z

time column parsed as `TIMESTAMP`

---

time 
2021-10-11T12:55Z

time column parsed as `VARCHAR`

### OS:

windows

### DuckDB Version:

@duckdb/duckdb-wasm@1.28.1-dev245.0

### DuckDB Client:

shell

### Full Name:

Dasha Kolmakova

### Affiliation:

-

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [ ] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE mixed_timestamps (id INT, time_column VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE parsed_timestamps AS SELECT id, time_column, CAST(time_column AS DATETIME) AS parsed_time FROM mixed_timestamps;
SELECT id, time_column, parsed_time FROM parsed_timestamps WHERE parsed_time IS NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM parsed_timestamps WHERE parsed_time IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Parsing mixed ISO 8601 timestamps as VARCHAR instead of TIMESTAMP
